rationale: The citing paper uses Hasford et al. (2015) as a key prior for emotional contagion and affect-as-information spillovers from adjacent persuasive content. It explicitly brings this citation into hypothesis-relevant sections (alternative-process testing in Study 3 and boundary-condition testing in Study 4a) to evaluate whether brand safety effects are merely affective contagion. The authors then report that trust erosion is the stronger mechanism and that unsafe content effects differ from merely negative content, positioning their contribution as a qualification of contagion-based explanations in this context.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Hasford et al. (2015) as the benchmark emotional contagion account, then narrows its scope for digital brand safety incidents. It shows that brand outcomes are better explained by trust erosion and by content unsafety, not just general negative affect transfer.
